Data exploration of European Tracking Network data inside European Offshore Wind Farms

Author

Lotte Pohl, lotte.pohl@ugent.be

TODO: - check with moratorium data -> how to present? - put the exact concession zone for deployments

Setup

workspace

R packages

# 0. data access
# install.packages("pak")
# library(pak)
# pak::pak("inbo/etn")
library(etn)
# library(DBI)
# library(RPostgreSQL)

# 1. data wrangling
library(dplyr)
library(purrr)
library(tidyr)
# library(glue)
library(forcats)
# library(magrittr)

# 2. spatiotemporal
library(lubridate)
library(sf)

# 3. visualisations
library(ggplot2)
library(plotly)
library(leaflet)
library(patchwork)
library(mapview)

# 4. analyses
# library(mgcv)

# 5. misc
library(knitr)
library(here)

working directory

Loading functions

source("R/helpers.R")
source("R/get_acoustic_detections.R")
source("R/utils_etn.R")

Setting paths

path_spatial <- "data/spatial"
path_data_raw <- "data_raw"
path_data_raw_etn <- "data_raw/etn"
path_data <- "data"
path_plots <- "docs/plots"

Loading data

load_files_to_env(path = path_spatial, file_ext = "gpkg")

spatial OWF data

ETN data

deployments of acoustic receivers

let’s see when and were the OWF deployments were

To explore what species have been detected in BE OWF, we get all belgian OWF stations and get the acoustic detections for those.

summary stats

TODO ideas: cumulative days of deployment, overall area covered, dist to coast, # of deployments

animal projects

Let’s check for relevant animal projects

animals

TODO: put somewhere else (doesn’t follow the flow of thought here)

Animals tagged in projects that have one of the following keywords in their name: elasmo, elasmobranch, seabass, mackerel, OWF, VLIZ, shark, ray:

detections

query acoustic detections at the acoustic receiver deployments inside OWF

Checking the acoustic detections at the stations that have had receivers deployed the longest

abacus plots per species

Some summary statistics

Seabass has the most detections inside Belgian OWF. So let’s look a bit more in detail into those detections

seabass

tests
